While enhanced bonding through etching and priming typically contributes positively to mechanical properties like bond strength and durability, it doesn’t directly affect the fundamental volume change during curing which leads to shrinkage. Therefore, statement A accurately reflects this nuance since increased surface area from improved bonding doesn’t automatically equate to reduced shrinkage; other factors must be considered simultaneously.
